subject: QSR and Food Service

private_opportunities.title: McDonald's opening 8,000 restaurants with major upgrades
private_opportunities.snippet:
## McDonald’s unveils massive global expansion

McDonald’s has unveiled an ambitious roadmap to expand its global footprint, with plans to launch 8,000 new restaurants by the end of 2026. This aggressive expansion is the first phase of a larger mission to operate at least 50,000 locations worldwide by 2027. The strategy focuses on capturing high-traffic areas and entering emerging markets while simultaneously modernizing existing sites to keep pace with new corporate standards. [...] ## McDonald’s rolls out major menu and beverage overhaul

As part of this aggressive footprint expansion, McDonald’s is also reimagining its menu to capture a wider variety of dining habits. The most notable change comes from the company’s “CosMc’s” spinoff concept, which focuses on the high-margin beverage market. Following successful pilot testing, CosMc’s-inspired drinks are slated to roll out to more than 500 locations.

The innovation extends beyond the drink station and into the kitchen, where the chain is doubling down on its most popular proteins. McDonald’s is significantly expanding its chicken lineup, reintroducing a variety of wraps and launching upgraded sandwiches to meet the growing consumer demand for poultry. [...] Furthermore, the chain is returning to its roots with the “Better Burger Initiative.” This program is a comprehensive effort to elevate the quality of its core products through improved preparation techniques. By utilizing fresher ingredients and implementing tighter food-handling standards, McDonald’s aims to ensure that its signature burgers are served hotter and juicier.

## McDonald’s modernizes checkout experience for customers
private_opportunities.bid_fit_summary: McDonald's is planning a significant global expansion with 8,000 new restaurants by 2026. PARTTEAM could potentially supply QSR self-order kiosks and digital signage solutions to enhance customer experience and streamline operations in these new locations.

private_opportunities.title: McDonald's plans 25 new locations across Ireland in major $175 ...
private_opportunities.snippet:
Spencer also added, "We plan that our 50th anniversary in Ireland next year will coincide with the opening of our 100th restaurant in Ireland."

McDonald's has already started this ambitious expansion plan with a recent opening. Just last month, its newest restaurant welcomed customers in New Ross—located in southwest County Wexford.

This is just the first of the fast food giant's five openings planned for 2026, though the locations of the other four restaurants haven't been revealed yet.

‌

### McDonald's further investment plans in existing Irish locations

While the news of McDonald’s future restaurants are sure to be exciting for customers across the country, the company already has 96 restaurants throughout Ireland, reported by The Irish Times. [...] ‌

# McDonald’s plans 25 new locations across Ireland in major $175 million expansion

## Ahead of its 50th birthday in Ireland in 2027, McDonald's has unveiled a €150 million (about $175 million) investment plan that includes five new restaurants per year and investments in current locations.

Natalie Nguyen Affiliate Lead & Commerce Writer

10:53 ET, 04 Mar 2026

McDonald's has announced that it's investing €150 million (about $175 million) in opening 25 new restaurants in Ireland over the next five years, which will create about 1,750 jobs.

‌ [...] These are currently independently owned and operated by 18 Irish franchisees and employ over 7,000 people. Fortunately, along with McDonald's Irish growth plan, those operations will also be receiving investments from the company.

‌

McDonald's funding will cover the recent €3 million (about $3.5 million) refurbishment of its Cork Douglas location, which is led by local franchisee Jim Ronayne and reopened at the end of 2025.

This refurbishment was highlighted at 'Beneath and Beyond the Golden Arches,' a special event that "celebrates McDonald's economic and community impact" in Cork and across Ireland, according to a company press release.

private_opportunities.title: McDonald's Announces New Growth Strategy
private_opportunities.snippet:
The Company expects digital sales to exceed $10 billion or nearly 20% of Systemwide sales across its top six markets in 2020. To further expand on its already significant digital presence and bring more speed and convenience, more personalization, and even better value for its customers, the Company expects to launch "MyMcDonald’s" across those top six markets by the end of 2021.

Delivery

Nearly 75% of the population across the Company’s top markets lives within three miles of a McDonald’s, and this advantage allows the Company to meet customers’ evolving needs for speed and convenience. In the past three years, McDonald’s has expanded the number of restaurants that offer delivery nine-fold, to about 28,000 restaurants.
private_opportunities.bid_fit_summary: McDonald's is expanding its digital sales strategy with the launch of 'MyMcDonald’s' across its top six markets. PARTTEAM could potentially sell self-ordering kiosks and digital signage solutions to enhance McDonald's customer experience and operational efficiency.

executive_takeaway: The biggest opportunity this week lies in McDonald's aggressive expansion plans, both in Ireland and globally. Leadership should prioritize initiating strategic discussions with McDonald's Ireland and their global procurement teams to position PARTTEAM as a key supplier of self-order kiosks and digital signage solutions. This aligns with our objective to grow in the QSR and Food Service market. Immediate action is recommended to capitalize on these opportunities, particularly in Ireland where the expansion is imminent.

── EXECUTIVE TAKEAWAY ───────────────────────────────
The biggest opportunity this week lies in McDonald's aggressive expansion plans, both in Ireland and globally. Leadership should prioritize initiating strategic discussions with McDonald's Ireland and their global procurement teams to position PARTTEAM as a key supplier of self-order kiosks and digital signage solutions. This aligns with our objective to grow in the QSR and Food Service market. Immediate action is recommended to capitalize on these opportunities, particularly in Ireland where the expansion is imminent.
